You are a swarm teammate working on issue kapow-5ai: "Update Makefile and docs for kapow binary".

ISSUE DETAILS:
Add to root Makefile:
- kapow-build — compile tools/importer binary as kapow
- kapow-test — run all importer tests (race detector)  
- kapow-test-unit — unit tests only (-short)

Update:
- tools/CLAUDE.md — add importer/kapow documentation
- Root CLAUDE.md — add kapow binary to commands table and common task recipes
- README.md — document the kapow data import tool

Done when: make kapow-build and make kapow-test-unit work from root.

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. First read the existing Makefile, tools/CLAUDE.md, CLAUDE.md, and README.md to understand current patterns
2. Make the changes following existing conventions
3. Test that make kapow-build and make kapow-test-unit work
4. When done, run bd update kapow-5ai --status in_progress, then commit your changes, then run bd close kapow-5ai
5. Report any anomalies or errors you encounter

Work in a git worktree to isolate your changes.

## Kapow Monorepo Makefile — delegates to component Makefiles
## Usage: make <target>

# Load .env if present — makes vars available to shell commands in recipes
-include .env
export VITE_SUPABASE_URL VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY

.PHONY: api-build api-run api-generate api-test api-test-unit \
        api-lint api-migrate-up api-migrate-down api-clean schema-gen \
        web-install web-dev web-build web-lint web-clean web-test web-test-coverage web-e2e web-test-e2e web-test-e2e-critical web-screenshots web-storybook \
        web-schema-sync \
        lint audit hadolint \
        smoke-test smoke-test-with-server \
        api-image-build web-image-build test-image-build test-image-run \
        tools-build tools-test \
        dev dev-clean dev-prune dev-db-up dev-db-down \
        compose-up compose-down compose-logs \
        test-quick test-standard test-full test-fixture \
        fixture-sql fixture-json \
        build-full \
        spec-gen \
        demo \
        help

## api-build: Build the API server binary
api-build:
	$(MAKE) -C api build

## build-full: Build single binary with embedded web frontend (API + SPA)
build-full: web-build
	$(MAKE) -C api build-full

## api-run: Run the API server
api-run:
	$(MAKE) -C api run

## api-generate: Run GraphQL code generation
api-generate:
	$(MAKE) -C api generate

## api-test: Run all API tests
api-test:
	$(MAKE) -C api test

## api-test-unit: Run API unit tests
api-test-unit:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-unit

## api-lint: Run linter on the API
api-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C api lint

## api-migrate-up: Apply database migrations
api-migrate-up:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-up

## api-migrate-down: Roll back last database migration
api-migrate-down:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-down

## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
api-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C api clean

## schema-gen: Generate common/schema/schema.sql from migration Up blocks
schema-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-schema.sh

## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
web-install:
	$(MAKE) -C web install

## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql → :8081)
web-dev:
	$(MAKE) -C web dev

## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
web-build:
	$(MAKE) -C web build

## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
web-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C web lint

## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
web-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C web clean

## web-test: Run web unit tests
web-test:
	$(MAKE) -C web test

## web-test-coverage: Run web unit tests with coverage report
web-test-coverage:
	$(MAKE) -C web test-coverage

## web-e2e: Run Playwright E2E tests for web frontend (starts Vite dev server automatically)
web-e2e:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test

## web-test-e2e: Run all Playwright E2E tests (alias with webServer auto-start)
web-test-e2e:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test

## web-test-e2e-critical: Run only @critical tier E2E tests
web-test-e2e-critical:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test-critical

## web-screenshots: Capture visual baseline screenshots across viewports
web-screenshots:
	$(MAKE) -C web screenshots

## web-storybook: Start Storybook dev server for UI components (port 6006)
web-storybook:
	$(MAKE) -C web storybook

## web-schema-sync: Regenerate web/schema.graphql and graphql-env.d.ts from API SDL files
web-schema-sync:
	cat api/graph/schema/schema.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/auth.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/data_sources.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/echo.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/field_names_gen.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/institution.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/preferences.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/similarity.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/zip.graphql \
	    > web/schema.graphql
	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## audit: Run security audit on web dependencies (non-zero exit on high+ severity)
audit:
	cd web && pnpm audit --audit-level=high

## hadolint: Lint all Dockerfiles with hadolint (runs via Docker)
hadolint:
	@find . -name 'Dockerfile*' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/dist/*' | sort | \
		while read -r f; do \
			echo "Linting $$f"; \
			docker run --rm -i hadolint/hadolint < "$$f" || exit 1; \
		done

## smoke-test: Run search smoke test against localhost:8081 (requires running API)
smoke-test:
	bash scripts/smoke-test.sh

## smoke-test-with-server: Build API, start it, run smoke test, tear down (requires DATABASE_URL)
smoke-test-with-server:
	bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh

## api-image-build: Build the standalone API production Docker image
api-image-build:
	docker build -f api/Dockerfile \
		--build-arg VERSION=$$(git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev) \
		--build-arg BUILD_TIME=$$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
		-t kapow-api \
		api/

## web-image-build: Build the standalone web frontend Docker image (nginx + Vite build)
web-image-build:
	docker build \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
		-t kapow-web web/

## test-image-build: Build the all-in-one test Docker image (postgres+API+nginx)
test-image-build: hadolint
	docker build \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
		-t kapow-test .

## test-image-run: Run the test image, exposing the web UI on http://localhost:8080
test-image-run: test-image-build
	docker run --rm -p 8080:80 kapow-test

## demo: Build test image, run it, and launch the interactive Playwright demo
demo:
	bash scripts/demo.sh $(DEMO_ARGS)

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (PostgreSQL + API + web) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-clean: Remove this checkout's dev database volume (forces fresh fixture load on next `make dev`)
dev-clean:
	@HASH=$$(echo "$$PWD" | md5sum | cut -c1-8); \
	VOL="kapow-dev-pgdata-$$HASH"; \
	CONT="kapow-dev-$$HASH"; \
	docker rm -f "$$CONT" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	if docker volume inspect "$$VOL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		docker volume rm "$$VOL"; \
		echo "Removed volume $$VOL"; \
	else \
		echo "Volume $$VOL does not exist"; \
	fi

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PostgreSQL+PostGIS on port 5433, full dataset)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## compose-up: Start 3-service production topology (db + api + web) on http://localhost:8080
compose-up:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml up -d --build --wait

## compose-down: Stop and remove compose services and volumes
compose-down:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml down -v

## compose-logs: Tail logs from all compose services
compose-logs:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml logs -f

## dev-prune: Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
dev-prune:
	@CUTOFF=$$(date -d '30 days ago' +%s 2>/dev/null || date -v-30d +%s); \
	for vol in $$(docker volume ls --filter label=kapow-dev=true -q 2>/dev/null); do \
		CREATED=$$(docker volume inspect "$$vol" --format '{{.CreatedAt}}' 2>/dev/null); \
		VOL_TS=$$(date -d "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0); \
		if [ "$$VOL_TS" -lt "$$CUTOFF" ]; then \
			echo "Removing old volume: $$vol (created $$CREATED)"; \
			docker volume rm "$$vol" 2>/dev/null || true; \
		fi; \
	done; \
	echo "Prune complete."

## tools-build: Build all tools (samplesql)
tools-build:
	cd tools/samplesql && go build -o ../../bin/samplesql .

## tools-test: Run tests for all tools
tools-test:
	cd tools/samplesql && go test ./...

## test-quick: Fast checks — Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick:
	MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation — quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard:
	$(MAKE) test-quick && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh web-test api-lint

## test-fixture: Validate fixture data loads cleanly against current schema (spins up temp Docker PG)
test-fixture:
	bash scripts/test-fixture.sh

## test-full: Complete validation — standard + integration, e2e, smoke, fixture (requires DB + running services)
test-full:
	$(MAKE) test-standard && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test test-fixture tools-test && $(MAKE) web-test-e2e && $(MAKE) smoke-test

## fixture-sql: Generate complete.sql.xz from JSON fixture data (requires Docker)
fixture-sql:
	bash scripts/gen-fixture-sql.sh

## fixture-json: Re-extract JSON fixture data from a live database (requires DATABASE_URL)
fixture-json:
	cd tools/samplesql && go run . --format json --output-dir ../../common/src/main/resources/data/tables

## spec-gen: Generate reference documentation in docs/specs/generated/
spec-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-specs.sh

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@ESSENTIAL="api-generate build-full dev help lint test-image-run test-full test-quick test-standard web-schema-sync"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Usage: make <target>"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Essential targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then \
					printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
					break; \
				fi; \
			done; \
		done; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "All targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			SKIP=false; \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then SKIP=true; break; fi; \
			done; \
			if [ "$$SKIP" = "false" ]; then \
				printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
			fi; \
		done

# Tools — Claude Instructions

Developer tools that operate on the Kapow database or codebase. Each tool is a
standalone Go module in its own subdirectory.

---

## samplesql

Extracts a deterministic sample of institution data from a live PostgreSQL
database and writes it as `COPY`-format SQL. Used to generate fixture data for
tests and the Docker test image.

**Module**: `github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/samplesql`

### How to run

```bash
cd tools/samplesql
go run . --database-url "$DATABASE_URL" --limit 100 --output sample.sql
```

### CLI flags

| Flag | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--database-url` | `DATABASE_URL` env var | PostgreSQL connection string |
| `--limit` | `100` | Number of institutions to sample |
| `--output` | stdout | Output file path |
| `--seed` | `42` | Random seed for deterministic sampling |

### Maintenance: updating samplesql after schema changes

1. **New table**: add an entry to the `tables` slice in `extractor.go →
   extractAll()`. Set `filtered: true` if the table has a `unit_id` column,
   `false` for lookup tables. Ensure FK-safe ordering (parent before child).
2. **New/renamed columns**: no code change needed — `SELECT *` picks up schema
   changes automatically. Verify by running the tool against the dev database.
3. **Regenerate fixture data**: run the tool against a populated database, then
   compress and commit the output to `common/src/main/resources/data/`.
4. **Run tests**: `cd tools/samplesql && go test ./...`

# Kapow — College Power Search

A college/university search platform with a Go API backend and React frontend.

## Quick Start with Docker (recommended)

The fastest way to try Kapow — a single Docker image bundles PostgreSQL, the API
server, and the web frontend with pre-loaded fixture data.

```bash
# Build the all-in-one image
make test-image-build

# Run it (exposes port 8080)
make test-image-run
```

Open **http://localhost:8080** in your browser. To run the interactive demo
against it, see [DEMO.md](DEMO.md).

The container includes:

- PostgreSQL 15 + PostGIS with pre-loaded college data
- Go API server on internal port 8081
- nginx reverse proxy serving the React frontend and proxying API requests

No configuration needed — the container handles everything on first boot,
including database initialization and fixture data loading.

To stop: press `Ctrl+C`.

---

## Devcontainer

See [.devcontainer/README.md](.devcontainer/README.md) for setup instructions
(GitHub Codespaces, VS Code, and CLI).

---

## Local Development Setup

### Prerequisites

- **Go** 1.23+
- **Node.js** 22+ with **pnpm** 9+ (`npm install -g pnpm`)
- **Docker** (for the dev database container)
- **psql** (PostgreSQL client) — `brew install libpq` on macOS, `apt install postgresql-client` on Linux

Optional:

- **golangci-lint** for linting
- **air** for standalone API hot-reload: `go install github.com/air-verse/air@latest` (`make dev` uses `go run` instead, no install needed)

### Quick start (recommended)

A single command starts everything — PostgreSQL in Docker, the Go API with hot
reload, and the Vite dev server:

```bash
make dev
```

This will:
- Start a PostgreSQL + PostGIS container on port **15432** (won't conflict with a host PostgreSQL)
- Load fixture data on first run (subsequent runs skip this step)
- Run database migrations
- Start the API with **air** (hot reload) on **:8081**
- Start the web dev server on **:8080** with HMR

Open **http://localhost:8080** and you're ready to go. Press **Ctrl+C** to stop
everything. The database volume persists between runs, so the next `make dev`
starts much faster.

```bash
make dev-clean    # remove this checkout's DB volume (forces fresh fixture load)
make dev-prune    # remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
```

### Manual setup (alternative)

If you prefer to manage PostgreSQL yourself:

#### 1. Configure environment

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Edit `.env` and set at minimum:

```
DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:password@localhost:5432/kapow?sslmode=disable
JWT_SECRET=change-me-to-a-long-random-secret
```

#### 2. Set up the database

Create the database, then run migrations:

```bash
createdb kapow
make api-migrate-up
```

To load fixture data (from `common/src/main/resources/data/`):

```bash
xz -dc common/src/main/resources/data/complete.sql.xz | psql kapow
```

Smaller subsets are available for faster loading:

- `subset-VT.sql.xz` — Vermont only
- `subset-DE_MA_VT.sql.xz` — Delaware, Massachusetts, Vermont

#### 3. Start the API

```bash
make api-run
```

The API listens on **localhost:8081**. Verify with:

```bash
curl http://localhost:8081/private/healthcheck | jq .
```

#### 4. Start the web frontend

In a separate terminal:

```bash
make web-install   # first time only
make web-dev
```

The dev server runs on **localhost:8080** and proxies `/graphql` and `/api`
requests to the API at :8081.

Open **http://localhost:8080**.

#### 5. Authentication with Supabase (optional)

The GraphQL endpoint (`/graphql`) requires a valid JWT. In development you can
use any HS256 secret via `JWT_SECRET`. For production (or to test real login
flows), connect a Supabase project.

##### Create a Supabase project

1. Go to [supabase.com/dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard) and create a
   new project (or use an existing one).
2. Note your **Project URL** (`https://<project-ref>.supabase.co`) and
   **anon public** key from **Settings → API**.

##### Configure API environment variables

In `.env`, set the JWT vars so the Go API can validate Supabase-issued tokens:

```
JWT_SECRET=<your-supabase-jwt-secret>
JWT_ISSUER=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1
JWT_AUDIENCE=authenticated
```

The JWT Secret is on **Settings → API → JWT Secret**. Alternatively, paste the
**JWT Public Key** (RS256) into `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` for asymmetric validation.

##### Configure frontend environment variables

The web frontend uses the Supabase JS SDK for login/signup. Add these to `.env`:

```
VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=<your-anon-key>
```

The `VITE_` prefix is required so Vite exposes them to browser code.

##### Enable OAuth providers

To support social login (Google, Apple, etc.):

1. In the Supabase dashboard, go to **Authentication → Providers**.
2. **Google**: Enable the Google provider, paste your Google OAuth **Client ID**
   and **Client Secret** (from [Google Cloud Console → Credentials](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials)),
   and add your Supabase callback URL as an authorized redirect URI in Google.
3. **Apple**: Enable the Apple provider, upload your **Services ID**, **Team ID**,
   **Key ID**, and **private key** (from
   [Apple Developer → Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources)).
   Add the Supabase callback URL to your Apple Sign In configuration.

Each provider's callback URL is shown in the Supabase dashboard when you enable
it (typically `https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/callback`).

##### How it works

On the first authenticated request, the API automatically creates a personal
team and membership for the user (using the JWT `sub` claim as the user ID).

---

## Project Structure

```
kapow/
├── api/          # Go API server (GraphQL, search engine, auth)
├── web/          # React + TypeScript + Vite frontend
├── common/       # Shared resources (generated schema, fixture data)
├── docker/       # Container config (nginx, supervisord, entrypoint)
├── scripts/      # Shell utilities (smoke tests, schema generation)
├── tools/        # Developer utilities (see below)
├── Dockerfile    # All-in-one multi-stage build
└── Makefile      # Root — delegates to api/ and web/
```

### tools/

Developer utilities that operate on the database or fixture data.

| Tool | Description |
|---|---|
| `tools/samplesql` | Deterministic institution sampler — connects to a live database, randomly selects N institutions (default 100, seed 42), extracts all dependent rows, and writes a self-contained SQL fixture file using `COPY` statements. Useful for generating small, reproducible test datasets. |

**samplesql usage:**

```bash
cd tools/samplesql
go run . --database-url "$DATABASE_URL" --limit 100 --seed 42 --output sample.sql
```

Flags: `--database-url` (or `DATABASE_URL` env var), `--limit` (number of institutions), `--seed` (deterministic random seed), `--output` (file path; defaults to stdout).

---

## Useful Commands

### Development

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `make dev` | Start full stack (DB + API + web) with hot reload |
| `make dev-clean` | Remove dev DB volume (fresh fixture load on next run) |
| `make dev-prune` | Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days |

### API

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `make api-run` | Start the API server |
| `make api-test` | Run all Go tests (with race detector) |
| `make api-test-unit` | Unit tests only (no DB required) |
| `make api-generate` | Regenerate GraphQL code after schema changes |
| `make api-migrate-up` | Apply pending database migrations |
| `make api-migrate-down` | Roll back the last migration |
| `make api-lint` | Run go vet + golangci-lint (includes gosec) |
| `make lint` | Run all linters (Go + web) |
| `make audit` | Security audit on web dependencies |

### Web

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `make web-dev` | Start Vite dev server with HMR |
| `make web-build` | TypeScript check + production build |
| `make web-test` | Run Vitest unit tests |
| `make web-lint` | ESLint (zero-warning policy) |
| `make web-e2e` | Playwright end-to-end tests |
| `make web-storybook` | Start Storybook on port 6006 |

### Smoke Tests

```bash
# Against a running API
bash scripts/smoke-test.sh

# Builds the API, starts it, tests, and tears down automatically
bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh
```

---

## Ports

| Service | Port |
|---|---|
| Web dev server | 8080 |
| API server | 8081 |
| Dev PostgreSQL | 15432 |
| Docker (all-in-one) | 8080 |
| Storybook | 6006 |

---

## Environment Variables

See `.env.example` for the full list. The essential ones:

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `DATABASE_URL` | Yes | PostgreSQL connection string |
| `JWT_SECRET` | Yes | Secret for HS256 token signing (or Supabase JWT secret) |
| `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` | No | RS256 PEM public key — overrides `JWT_SECRET` when set |
| `JWT_ISSUER` | No | Expected `iss` claim (e.g. `https://<ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1`) |
| `JWT_AUDIENCE` | No | Expected `aud` claim (e.g. `authenticated`) |
| `GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND` | No | Set `true` to enable GraphQL Playground at `/playground` |
| `LOG_LEVEL` | No | `debug`, `info` (default), `warn`, `error` |
| `LOG_FORMAT` | No | `json` (default) or `text` (dev) |
| `CORS_ORIGINS` | No | Allowed origins, e.g. `http://localhost:8080` |

# Kapow — Claude Instructions

College Power Search: a monorepo with a Go API (`api/`) and a React/TypeScript
frontend (`web/`). Component-specific deep-dives are in the `@import` files below.

---

## Prerequisites

- **Go** 1.23+
- **Node.js** 22+ with **pnpm** 9+ (`npm install -g pnpm`)
- **Docker** (for `make dev` — runs PostgreSQL in a container)
- **PostgreSQL** 15+ with PostGIS (only if managing DB manually instead of `make dev`)
- **golangci-lint** (optional): see golangci-lint docs

---

## Environment setup

```bash
cp .env.example .env   # fill in DATABASE_URL and JWT_SECRET at minimum
```

Required: `DATABASE_URL` — the API refuses to start without it.

Ports:
- **API**: `:8081`
- **Web dev server**: `:8080` — proxies `/graphql` and `/api` → `localhost:8081`

---

## Commands (Root Makefile)

| Command | Action |
|---|---|
| `make dev` | Full-stack dev environment (Docker PG + air + Vite) — single command |
| `make dev-clean` | Remove dev DB volume (forces fresh fixture load) |
| `make dev-prune` | Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days |
| `make api-build` | Compile `bin/kapow-api` |
| `make api-run` | Run API server |
| `make api-generate` | Regenerate gqlgen code |
| `make api-test` | All Go tests (race detector) |
| `make api-test-unit` | Unit tests only (`-short`) |
| `make lint` | Run all linters (Go + web) |
| `make audit` | Security audit on web dependencies |
| `make api-lint` | go vet + golangci-lint (includes gosec) |
| `make api-migrate-up` | Apply pending migrations |
| `make api-migrate-down` | Roll back last migration |
| `make schema-gen` | Rebuild `common/.../schema.sql` |
| `make web-install` | `pnpm install` |
| `make web-dev` | Vite dev server (port 8080) |
| `make web-build` | TypeScript check + production build |
| `make web-lint` | ESLint (zero-warning mode) |
| `make web-test` | Vitest unit tests |
| `make web-schema-sync` | Regenerate `web/schema.graphql` + `graphql-env.d.ts` from API SDL |
| `make web-clean` | Remove `dist/` and `node_modules/` |
| `make web-test-e2e` | All Playwright E2E tests (auto-starts dev server) |
| `make web-test-e2e-critical` | Only `@critical` tier E2E tests |
| `make smoke-test` | Search API smoke test (requires running API) |
| `make test-fixture` | Validate fixture data loads against current schema (temp Docker PG) |
| `make test-quick` | Fast checks: Go unit tests + TS build + ESLint (~10-20s, no DB) |
| `make test-standard` | Full local: quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB) |
| `make test-full` | Complete: standard + integration + e2e + smoke + fixture (requires DB + services) |
| `make test-image-build` | Build all-in-one Docker test image |
| `make test-image-run` | Run the Docker test image |
| `make spec-gen` | Regenerate `docs/specs/generated/` reference docs |

---

## Testing standards

### Test tiers

| Tier | Command | Time | External deps | Use when |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Quick | `make test-quick` | ~10-20s | None | During development |
| Standard | `make test-standard` | ~30-60s | None | Before committing |
| Full | `make test-full` | ~2-5min | DB + running services | Before merge |

### Core rules

- **Always write tests for new additions.** Tests ship with the code, not as a follow-up.
- **Update existing tests when modifying behaviour.**
- **Target ≥ 80% line coverage per package.** Glue/wiring packages (`cmd/server`, `router`) are exempt.
- **Always run database code against a real database.** Compilation and unit tests alone are not sufficient — runtime issues like pgx type encoding mismatches only surface with a live connection.

---

## Issue tracking (Beads)

```bash
bd list                                # all open issues
bd ready                               # issues with no blockers (ready to work)
bd show <id>                           # detailed view with dependencies
bd create --title "..." --description "..."
bd update <id> --status in_progress
bd close <id>
```

See `AGENTS.md` for issue type guidance, epic creation, dependencies, and
completion workflow.

---

## Common task recipes

### Add a config env var

1. Add field to `Config` struct in `api/internal/config/config.go` with `env:"VAR_NAME"` tag
2. Document it in `.env.example`
3. Access via `cfg.FieldName` (config flows through `router.Deps`)

### Add a database query

Use pgx directly: `pool.QueryRow(ctx, sql, $1, $2, ...)`. Always pass context.
Never build SQL with `fmt.Sprintf` — always use `$N` placeholders.

### Add a Go package

Create `api/internal/<name>/` with `package <name>`. Pass deps explicitly via
structs; no `init()` globals.

---

## Git workflow

When work is complete and verified, merge directly into `main` from the command
line. Do not create pull requests unless there is an unresolvable merge problem.

See `AGENTS.md` for commit conventions, worktree workflow, and session
completion protocol.

---

## Implementation plans

After plan mode approval, copy the plan from `~/.claude/plans/` into
`docs/plans/` with a meaningful name (e.g., `kapow-a1b-geo-search.md`).
Plans in `~/.claude/` are machine-local; the repo copy syncs across devices.
See `AGENTS.md` for naming conventions and lifecycle.

---

## Project specs (`docs/specs/`)

Comprehensive product and architecture documentation lives in `docs/specs/`.
Consult these before making significant changes to understand current behaviour.

**Hand-written specs** (edit when behaviour changes):

| File | Covers |
|---|---|
| `product-overview.md` | What Kapow is, features, user stories, pages |
| `graphql-api.md` | Queries, mutations, types, auth requirements |
| `search-engine.md` | Field types, SQL generation, themes, data sources |
| `data-model.md` | Schemas, tables, relationships, migrations |
| `authentication.md` | JWT flow, Supabase, middleware, protected routes |
| `frontend-architecture.md` | Pages, components, state, GraphQL client, styling |
| `deployment.md` | Docker images, compose, env vars, startup sequence |

**Auto-generated reference** (`docs/specs/generated/` — regenerate with `make spec-gen`):

| File | Source |
|---|---|
| `search-fields.md` | `search.NewRegistry()` — all fields grouped by theme |
| `data-sources.md` | `search.AllDataSources()` — all 12 data sources |
| `graphql-schema.md` | `api/graph/schema/*.graphql` — full SDL |
| `env-reference.md` | `.env.example` — all environment variables |

---

## What NOT to do

- **Never edit** `graph/generated/generated.go` or `graph/model/models_gen.go` — run `make api-generate`
- **Never commit** `.env` — only `.env.example`
- **Never use `fmt.Sprintf` to build SQL** — use parameterized `$N` placeholders
- **Never hardcode brand colors** — use Mantine color refs (`c="brand.9"`, `color="accent"`) or CSS vars (`var(--color-primary)` / `var(--color-accent)`)
- **Never use Tailwind utility classes** — use Mantine style props or inline styles
- **Never add** `node_modules/` or `dist/` to git

---

## README.md

`README.md` is the human-facing project documentation. **Keep it up to date**
when making changes that affect how someone builds, runs, or configures the
project.

---

@api/CLAUDE.md
@web/CLAUDE.md
@tools/CLAUDE.md
